# Define the graph with parallel timelines
graph <- grViz("

# Graph nodes (timelinediffQ events)
node [shape=box]

A [label='Ancient Times: Basic Arithmetic (Before 1000 BC)' tooltip='Basic arithmetic operations like addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division were known to ancient civilizations like the Egyptians and Babylonians']

B [label='Hellenistic Mathematics: Simple Equations (300 BC - 200 AD)' tooltip ='Greek mathematicians like Euclid and Diophantus began working with simple algebraic equations. Diophantus work in Arithmetica laid the foundation for algebra, although it was still in a rudimentary form.']

C [label='Islamic Golden Age: Algebra Develops (7th - 12th Century)' tooltip ='Algebra flourished under mathematicians like Al-Khwarizmi, whose works introduced methods of solving linear and quadratic equations. His book Al-Kitab al-Mukhtasar fi Hisab al-Jabr wal-Muqabala (The Compendious Book on Calculation by Completion and Balancing) is one of the earliest works on the subject']

D [label='Renaissance: Cubic and Quartic Equations (14th - 17th Century)' tooltip ='European mathematicians made significant advances. The solution of cubic and quartic equations was achieved in the 16th century by mathematicians like Scipione del Ferro, Niccolò Fontana Tartaglia, Gerolamo Cardano, and Ludovico Ferrari.']

E [label='Calculus and Differential Equations (17th Century)' tooltip ='With the development of calculus by Isaac Newton and Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz, the groundwork was laid for the formal study of differential equations. This period saw the first systematic methods for solving differential equations.']

F [label='Advances in Differential Equations Theory (18th Century)' tooltip ='Leonhard Euler and Joseph-Louis Lagrange made substantial contributions to the theory of differential equations. Eulers work on integrating factors and Lagranges method of variation of parameters were crucial in solving differential equations.']

G [label='Foundations of Complex Analysis (19th Century)' tooltip ='Augustin-Louis Cauchy and Bernhard Riemann developed rigorous foundations for complex analysis and contributed to the theory of differential equations. Karl Weierstrass and others formalized the foundations of analysis, which furthered understanding of differential equations.']

H [label='Computational Revolution (20th Century)' tooltip ='Advancements in computational power and algorithms have enabled the solving of extremely complex systems of equations and high-order differential equations, which are now commonplace in various fields of science and engineering']

I [label='Complex Equations in Science and Engineering (Late 20th - Early 21st Century)' tooltip ='Advancements in computational power and algorithms have enabled the solving of extremely complex systems of equations and high-order differential equations, which are now commonplace in various fields of science and engineering']
